long_description: A primary flight control actuator jams in flight, causing asymmetric control response, restricted maneuver authority, or increased pilot workload.
pilot_actions: Stop aggressive inputs, hold a controllable flight regime, use trim and remaining control authority, and recover for a straight-in landing if feasible.

long_description: Air data or flight control sensing becomes unreliable in flight, producing false speed, altitude, or control-law cues.
pilot_actions: Cross-check standby instruments, revert to alternate or degraded control mode if available, fly conservative pitch-power references, and divert.

long_description: A flight control computer lane fails or resets in flight, forcing control-law reversion, reduced envelope protection, or degraded handling quality.
pilot_actions: Acknowledge control-law change, stop aggressive maneuvering, maintain a stable regime, and recover using degraded-mode procedures.

long_description: Flight-control sensor channels disagree in flight, creating caution messages, control-law voting conflicts, or reduced autopilot confidence.
pilot_actions: Disengage automation if unstable, cross-check aircraft response, avoid edge-of-envelope maneuvering, and recover in a conservative configuration.

long_description: Pitch or roll trim runs away or commands persist unexpectedly in flight, increasing control force and risking loss of precise attitude control.
pilot_actions: Arrest the trim motion with override or cutout procedures, stabilize the aircraft manually, and recover before workload becomes excessive.
